root_text.sn44.8:1.1: Atha kho vacchagotto paribbājako yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavatā saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.sn44.8:1.2: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho vacchagotto paribbājako bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn44.8:1.3: “kiṁ nu kho, bho gotama, sassato loko”ti?
root_text.sn44.8:1.5: “Kiṁ pana, bho gotama, ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’”ti?
root_text.sn44.8:1.7: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’”ti.
root_text.sn44.8:2.1: “Ko nu kho, bho gotama, hetu, ko paccayo, yena aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ evaṁ puṭṭhānaṁ evaṁ veyyākaraṇaṁ hoti:
root_text.sn44.8:2.3: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’ti vā?
root_text.sn44.8:2.4: Ko pana, bho gotama, hetu, ko paccayo, yena bhoto gotamassa evaṁ puṭṭhassa na evaṁ veyyākaraṇaṁ hoti:
root_text.sn44.8:2.6: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipī”ti?
root_text.sn44.8:3.1: “Aññatitthiyā kho, vaccha, paribbājakā rūpaṁ attato samanupassanti, rūpavantaṁ vā attānaṁ, attani vā rūpaṁ, rūpasmiṁ vā attānaṁ.
root_text.sn44.8:3.5: viññāṇaṁ attato samanupassanti, viññāṇavantaṁ vā attānaṁ, attani vā viññāṇaṁ, viññāṇasmiṁ vā attānaṁ.
root_text.sn44.8:3.6: Tasmā aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ evaṁ puṭṭhānaṁ evaṁ veyyākaraṇaṁ hoti:
root_text.sn44.8:3.8: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’ti vā.
root_text.sn44.8:3.9: Tathāgato ca kho, vaccha, arahaṁ sammāsambuddho na rūpaṁ attato samanupassati, na rūpavantaṁ vā attānaṁ, na attani vā rūpaṁ, na rūpasmiṁ vā attānaṁ.
root_text.sn44.8:3.13: na viññāṇaṁ attato samanupassati, na viññāṇavantaṁ vā attānaṁ, na attani vā viññāṇaṁ, na viññāṇasmiṁ vā attānaṁ.
root_text.sn44.8:3.14: Tasmā tathāgatassa evaṁ puṭṭhassa na evaṁ veyyākaraṇaṁ hoti:
root_text.sn44.8:3.16: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipī”ti.
root_text.sn44.8:4.1: Atha kho vacchagotto paribbājako uṭṭhāyāsanā yenāyasmā mahāmoggallāno tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmatā mahāmoggallānena saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.sn44.8:4.2: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho vacchagotto paribbājako āyasmantaṁ mahāmoggallānaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn44.8:4.3: “kiṁ nu kho, bho moggallāna, sassato loko”ti?
root_text.sn44.8:4.5: “Kiṁ pana, bho moggallāna, ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’”ti?
root_text.sn44.8:4.7: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’”ti.
root_text.sn44.8:5.1: “Ko nu kho, bho moggallāna, hetu, ko paccayo, yena aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ evaṁ puṭṭhānaṁ evaṁ veyyākaraṇaṁ hoti:
root_text.sn44.8:5.3: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’ti vā?
root_text.sn44.8:5.4: Ko pana, bho moggallāna, hetu, ko paccayo yena samaṇassa gotamassa evaṁ puṭṭhassa na evaṁ veyyākaraṇaṁ hoti:
root_text.sn44.8:5.6: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipī”ti?
root_text.sn44.8:6.1: “Aññatitthiyā kho, vaccha, paribbājakā rūpaṁ attato samanupassanti, rūpavantaṁ vā attānaṁ, attani vā rūpaṁ, rūpasmiṁ vā attānaṁ.
root_text.sn44.8:6.5: viññāṇaṁ attato samanupassanti, viññāṇavantaṁ vā attānaṁ, attani vā viññāṇaṁ, viññāṇasmiṁ vā attānaṁ.
root_text.sn44.8:6.6: Tasmā aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ evaṁ puṭṭhānaṁ evaṁ veyyākaraṇaṁ hoti:
root_text.sn44.8:6.8: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’ti vā.
root_text.sn44.8:6.9: Tathāgato ca kho, vaccha, arahaṁ sammāsambuddho na rūpaṁ attato samanupassati, na rūpavantaṁ vā attānaṁ, na attani vā rūpaṁ, na rūpasmiṁ vā attānaṁ.
root_text.sn44.8:6.13: na viññāṇaṁ attato samanupassati, na viññāṇavantaṁ vā attānaṁ, na attani vā viññāṇaṁ, na viññāṇasmiṁ vā attānaṁ.
root_text.sn44.8:6.14: Tasmā tathāgatassa evaṁ puṭṭhassa na evaṁ veyyākaraṇaṁ hoti:
root_text.sn44.8:6.15: ‘sassato loko’tipi, ‘asassato loko’tipi, ‘antavā loko’tipi, ‘anantavā loko’tipi, ‘taṁ jīvaṁ taṁ sarīran’tipi, ‘aññaṁ jīvaṁ aññaṁ sarīran’tipi, ‘hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipi, ‘na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipi, ‘hoti ca na ca hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipi, ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipī”ti.
root_text.sn44.8:7.2: Yatra hi nāma satthu ca sāvakassa ca atthena attho byañjanena byañjanaṁ saṁsandissati, samessati, na virodhayissati, yadidaṁ aggapadasmiṁ.
root_text.sn44.8:7.3: Idānāhaṁ, bho moggallāna, samaṇaṁ gotamaṁ upasaṅkamitvā etamatthaṁ apucchiṁ.
root_text.sn44.8:7.4: Samaṇopi me gotamo etehi padehi etehi byañjanehi etamatthaṁ byākāsi, seyyathāpi bhavaṁ moggallāno.
root_text.sn44.8:7.5: Acchariyaṁ, bho moggallāna, abbhutaṁ, bho moggallāna.
root_text.sn44.8:7.6: Yatra hi nāma satthu ca sāvakassa ca atthena attho byañjanena byañjanaṁ saṁsandissati samessati na virodhayissati, yadidaṁ aggapadasmin”ti.

translation_text.sn44.8:1.1: Then the wanderer Vacchagotta went up to the Buddha and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.sn44.8:1.2: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side, and said to the Buddha:
translation_text.sn44.8:1.3: “Worthy Gotama, is this right: ‘the cosmos is eternal’?”
translation_text.sn44.8:1.4: “This has not been declared by me, Vaccha.” …
translation_text.sn44.8:1.5: “Then is this right: ‘a realized one neither still exists nor no longer exists after death’?”
translation_text.sn44.8:1.6: “This too has not been declared by me.”
translation_text.sn44.8:2.1: “What’s the cause, worthy Gotama, what’s the reason why, when the wanderers of other religions are asked these questions, they declare one of these to be true?
translation_text.sn44.8:2.4: And what’s the reason why, when the worthy Gotama is asked these questions, he does not declare one of these to be true?”
translation_text.sn44.8:3.1: “Vaccha, the wanderers of other religions regard form as self, self as having form, form in self, or self in form.
translation_text.sn44.8:3.5: consciousness as self, self as having consciousness, consciousness in self, or self in consciousness.
translation_text.sn44.8:3.6: That’s why, when asked, they declare one of those answers to be true.
translation_text.sn44.8:3.9: The Realized One doesn’t regard form as self, self as having form, form in self, or self in form.
translation_text.sn44.8:3.13: consciousness as self, self as having consciousness, consciousness in self, or self in consciousness.
translation_text.sn44.8:3.14: That’s why, when asked, he does not declare one of those answers to be true.”
translation_text.sn44.8:4.1: Then the wanderer Vacchagotta got up from his seat and went to Venerable Mahāmoggallāna, and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.sn44.8:4.2: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side. He asked Mahāmoggallāna the same questions, and received the same answers.
translation_text.sn44.8:7.1: He said, “It’s incredible, worthy Moggallāna, it’s amazing.
translation_text.sn44.8:7.2: How the meaning and the phrasing of the teacher and the disciple fit together and agree without contradiction when it comes to the chief matter!
translation_text.sn44.8:7.3: Just now I went to the ascetic Gotama and asked him about this matter.
translation_text.sn44.8:7.4: And he explained it to me with these words and phrases, just like the worthy Moggallāna.
translation_text.sn44.8:7.5: It’s incredible, worthy Moggallāna, it’s amazing!
translation_text.sn44.8:7.6: How the meaning and the phrasing of the teacher and the disciple fit together and agree without contradiction when it comes to the chief matter!”
